 {"id":"caam-0gy","title":"Session resume support for Codex profiles","description":"# Session Resume for Profile Isolation Mode

## IMPORTANT: Primary Workflow Already Works!

For the main "hit limit → switch → resume" workflow, **no new caam command is needed**:

\`\`\`bash
# Using Auth File Swapping mode (PRIMARY)
codex                              # Uses ~/.codex/
# ... hit limit, get session ID ...
caam activate codex backup         # Swaps auth file only
codex resume <session_id> "proceed"  # WORKS - sessions still in ~/.codex/
\`\`\`

Sessions stay in \`~/.codex/sessions/\` - only the auth file changes. Native \`codex resume\` works perfectly.

## When This Task Would Be Useful

This task is only needed for **Profile Isolation mode** (\`caam exec\`), where:
- Each profile has its own CODEX_HOME
- Sessions are stored per-profile, not shared
- Cross-profile resume wouldn't work without this

## Deprioritized

Since the primary workflow already works, this is P3 (nice-to-have for advanced users using profile isolation).

## If Implemented

\`\`\`bash
# For profile isolation mode only
caam resume codex work              # Resume last session for isolated profile
caam resume codex work --last       # Same, explicit
caam resume codex work "proceed"    # With prompt
\`\`\`

Would set CODEX_HOME to profile's directory, then run \`codex resume --last\`.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T20:12:30.022248297-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T20:37:15.123968209-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T20:37:15.123968209-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented caam resume for codex profiles + exec session ID capture persisted to profile.json (last_session_id/ts) with tests.","comments":[{"id":26,"issue_id":"caam-0gy","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting: implement codex session ID capture in `caam exec` + new `caam resume codex <profile>` command w/ stored last session ID in profile metadata (profile.json). -{"id":"caam-103","title":"Implement CLI import command","description":"# Task: Implement CLI import command

## Context
Implement `caam import` command to restore vault from a previously exported bundle.

## Command Specification

### Usage
```bash
caam import <bundle.zip> [flags]

Flags:
  --mode MODE           Import mode: smart (default), merge, or replace
  --password PASS       Password for encrypted bundles (or prompts)
  --dry-run             Preview what would be imported without changes
  --skip-config         Don't import config.yaml
  --skip-projects       Don't import projects.json
  --skip-health         Don't import health.json
  --skip-database       Don't import caam.db
  --skip-sync           Don't import sync configuration
  --providers PROVIDERS Comma-separated list of providers to import
  --profiles PROFILES   Comma-separated list of profile patterns to import
  --force               Skip confirmation prompts
  --json                Output result as JSON
```

### Import Modes

#### Smart Mode (default) ⭐
Uses **freshness comparison** for conflicts:
- New profiles: add to vault
- Existing profiles: compare token expiry, keep fresher one
- This is the intelligent default that preserves the best tokens

#### Merge Mode
Conservative approach:
- New profiles: add to vault
- Existing profiles: skip (keep local, don't overwrite)
- Use when you want to add missing profiles without touching existing

#### Replace Mode
Aggressive approach:
- Overwrite all matching profiles from bundle
- Does NOT delete local profiles not in bundle
- Use for restoring from a known-good backup

### Examples
```bash
# Import with smart freshness comparison (default)
caam import ~/backup.zip

# Preview what would happen
caam import ~/backup.zip --dry-run

# Import encrypted bundle
caam import ~/backup.enc.zip --password "secret"

# Conservative merge (never overwrite)
caam import ~/backup.zip --mode merge

# Force overwrite from bundle
caam import ~/backup.zip --mode replace --force

# Import only specific providers
caam import ~/backup.zip --providers claude,codex

# Skip optional files
caam import ~/backup.zip --skip-config --skip-database
```

## Implementation

### Location
`cmd/caam/cmd/import.go` (new file)

### Flow
1. Validate bundle path exists and is readable
2. Check if encrypted → prompt for password if needed
3. Extract to temp directory (decrypt if needed)
4. Parse and validate manifest.json
5. Verify checksums (warn on mismatch, fail on critical files)
6. Check version compatibility
7. If --dry-run, display import preview and exit
8. Confirm with user (unless --force)
9. Import vault contents based on mode:
   - Smart: compare freshness, keep better token
   - Merge: skip existing
   - Replace: overwrite from bundle
10. Import optional files if not skipped
11. Clean up temp directory
12. Print success message with stats

### Import Preview (--dry-run)
```
Import Preview: ~/backup.zip

Bundle Info:
  Created: December 19, 2025 at 2:29 PM
  Source: jeffs-macbook (darwin/arm64)
  CAAM Version: 1.2.3
  Encrypted: no

Profiles to Import (smart mode):
  claude:
    alice@gmail.com - NEW (will be added)
    bob@gmail.com - CONFLICT:
      Local expires:  2025-12-20 10:00:00
      Bundle expires: 2025-12-21 14:30:00  ← FRESHER (will import)
  codex:
    work@company.com - NEW (will be added)

Optional Files:
  config.yaml - will be merged
  projects.json - will be merged (3 new associations)
  health.json - will be imported
  sync/pool.json - will be merged
  caam.db - not included in bundle

Summary:
  New profiles: 2
  Updated profiles: 1 (fresher in bundle)
  Skipped profiles: 0
```

### Smart Mode Freshness Comparison
```go
func shouldImportProfile(localPath, bundlePath string) (bool, string) {
    localFresh, err := extractFreshness(localPath)
    if err != nil {
        // Can't parse local - import bundle version
        return true, "local token unreadable"
    }
    
    bundleFresh, err := extractFreshness(bundlePath)
    if err != nil {
        // Can't parse bundle - keep local
        return false, "bundle token unreadable"
    }
    
    if bundleFresh.ExpiresAt.After(localFresh.ExpiresAt) {
        return true, "bundle token fresher"
    }
    return false, "local token fresher or equal"
}
```

### Confirmation Dialog
```
Import 2 new profiles and update 1 existing profile? (y/N)
```

### Encrypted Bundle Handling
```go
if bundle.IsEncrypted(bundlePath) {
    password := getPassword(cmd) // from flag or prompt
    if password == "" {
        return fmt.Errorf("encrypted bundle requires password")
    }
    // Decrypt and continue
}
```

### Version Compatibility
- Same major version: full compatibility
- Different major version: warn user, require --force
- Future version: fail with "Bundle requires newer caam version"

### Error Handling
- File not found: "Bundle not found: <path>"
- Invalid zip: "Invalid bundle: not a valid zip file"
- Missing manifest: "Invalid bundle: manifest.json not found"
- Corrupt manifest: "Invalid bundle: manifest.json is corrupt"
- Checksum mismatch: "Warning: checksum mismatch for <file>. Continue? (y/N)"
- Version incompatible: "Bundle was created with caam vX.Y.Z, which is incompatible"
- Decryption failed: "Failed to decrypt bundle: wrong password?"

## Code Structure

```go
var importCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "import <bundle.zip>",
    Short: "Import vault from bundle",
    Long:  `Restore saved auth profiles from a previously exported bundle.`,
    Args:  cobra.ExactArgs(1),
    RunE:  runImport,
}

func init() {
    importCmd.Flags().String("mode", "smart", "Import mode: smart, merge, or replace")
    importCmd.Flags().String("password", "", "Password for encrypted bundles")
    // ...
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Command registered with correct usage
- [ ] Validates bundle before import
- [ ] Detects and handles encrypted bundles
- [ ] Password prompt works for encrypted bundles
- [ ] Checksum verification works
- [ ] Smart mode uses freshness comparison correctly
- [ ] Merge mode adds new, skips existing
- [ ] Replace mode overwrites existing
- [ ] --dry-run shows accurate preview with freshness info
- [ ] Confirmation prompt works (and --force bypasses)
- [ ] Optional file importing works with skip flags
- [ ] Provider/profile filters work
- [ ] Cross-platform path handling works
- [ ] Error messages are helpful and actionable
- [ ] Unit tests for import logic
- [ ] Integration test: export → import roundtrip

## Files to Create/Modify
- Create: `cmd/caam/cmd/import.go`
- Create: `internal/bundle/import.go`
- Create: `internal/bundle/import_test.go`

## Dependencies
- Depends on: caam-4b5 (bundle format)
- Depends on: caam-x4j (export command - for testing roundtrip)
- Part of epic: caam-9y2 (Export/Import Vault Bundle)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BluePond","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:41:19.483692059-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T16:41:30.617167008-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-103","depends_on_id":"caam-4b5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:46:37.308208058-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-103","depends_on_id":"caam-x4j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:46:42.414033513-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"caam-108","title":"Switch delay system with jitter","description":"Add configurable delays to profile activation to reduce detection patterns.

## The Problem
Instant account switching creates a detectable pattern - account A hits limit at 14:32:15, account B becomes active at 14:32:16. This is obviously automated behavior.

## Solution
Add a delay before activation completes:
1. Calculate delay: random(min_seconds, max_seconds) + random(0, jitter_seconds)
2. Show countdown with progress bar
3. Allow Ctrl+C to skip (for emergencies)
4. Only applies when stealth.enabled = true

## User Experience
```
$ caam activate claude work-account

Stealth mode: waiting 73 seconds before switch...
[████████████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░] 73s remaining (Ctrl+C to skip)

Activated claude profile 'work-account'
```

If user presses Ctrl+C:
```
Skipping delay...
Activated claude profile 'work-account'
```

## Implementation
1. Add delay calculation helper in internal/stealth/delay.go
2. Add countdown display helper (reusable for other features)
3. Modify activate command to check stealth.enabled and apply delay
4. Handle Ctrl+C gracefully (don't abort entire command)

## Jitter Explanation
The jitter is ADDED to the base delay to make timing unpredictable:
- Without jitter: delays are always 30-120 seconds
- With 30s jitter: delays are 30-150 seconds, with extra randomness

This prevents 'delay fingerprinting' where the delay pattern itself becomes detectable.

## Files
- internal/stealth/delay.go: Delay calculation and countdown display
- internal/stealth/delay_test.go: Tests
- cmd/caam/cmd/activate.go: Integration","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:46:52.478804946-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:46:56.708118413-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T23:46:56.708118413-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented stealth switch delay: internal/stealth ComputeDelay+Wait (countdown/progress, skip channel) with tests; activate applies delay when stealth.switch_delay.enabled and Ctrl+C skips without aborting activation.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-108","depends_on_id":"caam-bgz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:47:03.147530503-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":57,"issue_id":"caam-108","author":"ubuntu","text":"Claimed. Implementing switch-delay stealth feature using current config schema (stealth.switch_delay.{enabled,min_seconds,max_seconds,show_countdown}) with random delay + countdown/progress and Ctrl+C to skip. Will add internal/stealth helpers + tests and integrate into activate.","created_at":"2025-12-18T04:43:03Z"}]}

 {"id":"caam-1ba","title":"Additional CLI Commands","description":"## Overview
Polish commands that improve the CLI experience for power users.

## Commands to Add
1. `init` - Initialize caam, create directories, guided setup
2. `which` - Show current default profile for each provider
3. `use` - Set default profile for a provider
4. `env` - Print environment variables for shell eval
5. `open` - Open provider account page in browser
6. `doctor` - Diagnose setup issues
7. `logout` - Logout from isolated profile (complement to existing `clear`)

## Priority
These are nice-to-have polish features. Core functionality works without them.

## User Value
- `use`/`which`: Less typing for frequent users
- `env`: Shell integration for advanced workflows
- `doctor`: Self-service troubleshooting
- `open`: Quick access to account management","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:53:55.263150841-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:40:41.684230005-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:40:41.684230005-05:00","close_reason":"All child tasks completed: init, use/which, env, open, doctor. The logout functionality is already available via existing commands."}

 {"id":"caam-ypx","title":"Add browser profile config to Profile struct","description":"## Task
Add fields to the Profile struct (internal/profile/profile.go) to store browser configuration:
- BrowserCommand: string (e.g., 'google-chrome', 'firefox', or full path)
- BrowserProfileDir: string (e.g., 'Profile 1' for Chrome, or full path)
- BrowserProfileName: string (human-friendly name like 'Work Google')

## Rationale
These fields allow each caam profile to be associated with a specific browser profile. When OAuth flows need to open a browser, we can launch the correct browser profile that already has the right Google/GitHub account logged in.

## Implementation Notes
- Add to Profile struct in internal/profile/profile.go
- Add to JSON serialization
- Consider making these optional (empty = use system default)
- Update profile.json schema

## Acceptance Criteria
- Profile struct has browser config fields
- Fields serialize/deserialize correctly
- Existing profiles without these fields still load (backwards compatible)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:50:00.728787825-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:14:47.055352233-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:14:47.055352233-05:00","close_reason":"Added BrowserCommand, BrowserProfileDir, BrowserProfileName fields to Profile struct with omitempty tags for backwards compatibility.  {"id":"caam-zha","title":"Profile export/import for cross-machine transfer","description":"# Profile Export/Import for Cross-Machine Transfer

## What

Add \`caam export\` and \`caam import\` commands to easily transfer profiles between machines.

## Why - This is the UNIVERSAL headless solution

The most caam-native way to set up headless servers:

1. Login on machine with browser (laptop, workstation)
2. \`caam export codex/work > profile.tar.gz\`
3. \`scp profile.tar.gz user@server:~\`
4. On server: \`caam import profile.tar.gz\`
5. \`caam activate codex work\` - instant, no login needed!

This works for **ALL providers** (Codex, Claude, Gemini) because it transfers the auth FILES, not worrying about login mechanisms.

## Commands

### caam export

\`\`\`bash
# Export single profile
caam export codex/work > codex-work.tar.gz
caam export codex work -o codex-work.tar.gz

# Export all profiles for a tool
caam export codex --all > codex-all.tar.gz

# Export entire vault
caam export --all > full-vault.tar.gz
\`\`\`

### caam import

\`\`\`bash
# Import from file
caam import codex-work.tar.gz

# Import from stdin (for piping)
cat codex-work.tar.gz | caam import -

# Import with rename
caam import codex-work.tar.gz --as codex/server-work
\`\`\`

## Implementation

### Export format

Tar.gz containing:
- Profile directory structure from vault
- Metadata file with export timestamp, source machine, caam version
- Checksum for integrity

### File: cmd/caam/cmd/export.go

\`\`\`go
var exportCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "export <tool/profile>",
    Short: "Export profile(s) for transfer to another machine",
    Long: \`Export profile auth files for transfer to another machine.

This is the recommended way to set up profiles on headless servers:
1. Login normally on a machine with a browser
2. Export the profile: caam export codex/work > profile.tar.gz
3. Transfer to headless server: scp profile.tar.gz server:
4. Import on server: caam import profile.tar.gz
5. Activate: caam activate codex work

The exported file contains only the auth credentials, not session state.\`,
    RunE: runExport,
}
\`\`\`

## Security Considerations

- Exported files contain sensitive OAuth tokens
- Recommend encrypting with gpg for transfer: \`caam export ... | gpg -c > profile.tar.gz.gpg\`
- Add --encrypt flag for built-in encryption (nice-to-have)
- Document that exported tokens should be treated like passwords

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] \`caam export codex/work\` produces valid tar.gz
- [ ] \`caam import profile.tar.gz\` restores profile correctly
- [ ] Imported profile can be activated immediately
- [ ] Works for all providers (codex, claude, gemini)
- [ ] Handles name conflicts gracefully (--force or --as)

## Why This is Better Than Device Code

| Aspect | Device Code | Export/Import |
|--------|-------------|---------------|
| Works for Codex | ✅ | ✅ |
| Works for Claude | ❌ (buggy setup-token) | ✅ |
| Works for Gemini | ⚠️ (recent, untested) | ✅ |
| Leverages caam's core strength | ❌ | ✅ |
| One-time setup | Login each server | Login once, transfer |
| Implementation complexity | Medium | Low |","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T18:05:19.598114513-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T18:43:22.039830895-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T18:43:22.039830895-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented `caam export`/`caam import` for vault transfer (tar.gz) with manifest + sha256 checksums, conflict handling (--force, --as), atomic import via temp dir + rename, and unit tests. Added Vault.BasePath() accessor.","comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"caam-zha","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting work on export/import. Plan: add  +  commands (tar.gz), include metadata + checksum, handle stdin/stdout and name conflicts, add tests. Will keep it minimal + safe (tokens treated as secrets).","created_at":"2025-12-17T23:30:39Z"},{"id":5,"issue_id":"caam-zha","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting work on export/import. Plan: add `caam export` + `caam import` commands (tar.gz), include metadata + checksum, handle stdin/stdout and name conflicts, add tests. Tokens are secrets; docs will recommend encrypting tarballs.","created_at":"2025-12-17T23:30:53Z"},{"id":7,"issue_id":"caam-zha","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented export/import and landed to main. Usage: `caam export codex/work > profile.tar.gz` (or `-o file`), `caam export --all > full-vault.tar.gz`, then on target machine `caam import profile.tar.gz` (or `cat ... | caam import -`). Import verifies SHA256 checksums and is atomic per profile; supports `--force` and single-profile rename via `--as codex/server-work`.","created_at":"2025-12-17T23:44:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"caam-zti","title":"Smart auto-backup before switch","description":"Prevent accidental data loss when switching profiles.

## The Problem
User modifies their auth (e.g., logs into new account in browser), then runs `caam activate foo`. Their unsaved changes are lost because the current state doesn't match any vault profile.

## Solution
Before restoring a profile, check if current auth state is 'unsaved':
1. Hash current auth files
2. Compare with all vault profiles
3. If NO MATCH → current state would be lost
4. Auto-backup to `_backup_<YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS>` profile
5. Continue with normal activate

## Config Option
```yaml
safety:
  auto_backup_before_switch: smart  # 'always' | 'smart' | 'never'
  max_auto_backups: 5  # Keep last N auto-backups, delete older
```

- 'smart': Only backup if state doesn't match any profile (default)
- 'always': Always backup before switch (creates many profiles)
- 'never': Skip (for power users who know what they're doing)

## Auto-backup Rotation
When max_auto_backups exceeded:
1. List all _backup_* profiles for the tool
2. Sort by timestamp (oldest first)
3. Delete oldest until count <= max

## Implementation
1. Add Vault.CurrentStateMatchesAny(fileSet) method
2. Add Vault.BackupCurrent(fileSet) -> '_backup_<timestamp>'
3. Add auto-backup rotation logic
4. Modify activate command to check config and call BackupCurrent
5. Print: 'Auto-backed up current state to _backup_20251217_143022'

## Files
- internal/authfile/authfile.go: New methods
- cmd/caam/cmd/activate.go: Add smart backup logic
- internal/config/spm_config.go: Add config options","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:44:52.070068986-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:31:15.970213702-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T23:31:15.970213702-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented smart auto-backup before switch. Adds BackupCurrent() for timestamped backups, RotateAutoBackups() for cleanup, and integrates with activate command based on safety config (always|smart|never modes).","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-zti","depends_on_id":"caam-nh8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:45:04.238174857-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-zti","depends_on_id":"caam-3w5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:45:09.347228092-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":52,"issue_id":"caam-zti","author":"ubuntu","text":"[ubuntu at 2025-12-18]
FYI caam-nh8 is now closed: `_` prefix reserved for system vault profiles; delete requires --force; ls shows [system]; meta.json now includes {type, created_by}. Useful for _backup_* rotation work.","created_at":"2025-12-18T03:48:31Z"}]}
-{"id":"caam-zxk","title":"Implement auto-sync triggering after auth changes","description":"# Task: Implement auto-sync triggering after auth changes

## Context
When auth is refreshed or backed up locally, automatically push the fresh token to all machines in the sync pool. This is the "push-based, not polling" requirement.

**IMPORTANT**: Auto-sync is OPT-IN. Users must explicitly enable it with `caam sync enable`.

## Trigger Points

### After Backup
When `caam backup <provider> <profile>` completes successfully:
1. Check if auto-sync is enabled
2. If yes, trigger sync for that specific profile

### After Token Refresh
When token is refreshed (via `caam refresh` or proactive refresh):
1. Check if auto-sync is enabled
2. If yes, trigger sync for that specific profile

### After Activate (when token changes)
When `caam activate` results in a token update:
1. Check if auto-sync is enabled
2. If yes, trigger sync

## Implementation

### Sync Hook Function
```go
package sync

// TriggerSyncIfEnabled checks if auto-sync is enabled and triggers sync
// Returns immediately - sync runs in background
func TriggerSyncIfEnabled(provider, profile string) {
    state, err := LoadSyncState()
    if err != nil {
        return // Sync not configured, silently ignore
    }
    
    // CRITICAL: Both must be enabled
    if !state.Pool.Enabled || !state.Pool.AutoSync {
        return // Sync disabled, do nothing
    }
    
    if len(state.Pool.Machines) == 0 {
        return // No machines to sync with
    }
    
    // Check throttle to prevent sync storms
    if !state.Throttler.ShouldSync(provider, profile) {
        return // Recently synced, skip
    }
    
    // Run sync in background goroutine
    go func() {
        ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Minute)
        defer cancel()
        
        syncer := NewSyncer(state)
        results, err := syncer.SyncProfile(ctx, provider, profile)
        
        // Log results
        logSyncResults(results, err)
        
        // Queue failures for retry
        if err != nil || hasFailures(results) {
            state.Queue.Add(provider, profile, getFailedMachines(results))
            state.Save()
        }
        
        // Update throttle timestamp
        state.Throttler.RecordSync(provider, profile)
    }()
}
```

### Integration with Backup Command
In `cmd/caam/cmd/backup.go`:
```go
func runBackup(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    // ... existing backup logic ...
    
    if err := vault.Backup(provider, profileName); err != nil {
        return err
    }
    
    fmt.Printf("Backed up %s auth to profile '%s'\n", provider, profileName)
    
    // Trigger sync if enabled (non-blocking)
    sync.TriggerSyncIfEnabled(provider, profileName)
    
    return nil
}
```

### Integration with Refresh Command
In `cmd/caam/cmd/refresh.go`:
```go
func runRefresh(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    // ... existing refresh logic ...
    
    if err := refresher.Refresh(provider, profileName); err != nil {
        return err
    }
    
    fmt.Printf("Refreshed token for %s/%s\n", provider, profileName)
    
    // Trigger sync if enabled (non-blocking)
    sync.TriggerSyncIfEnabled(provider, profileName)
    
    return nil
}
```

### Sync Queue for Retries
When sync fails (machine offline, network error), queue for retry:

```go
type SyncQueue struct {
    Entries []QueueEntry `json:"entries"`
    MaxSize int          `json:"max_size"` // Default: 100
}

type QueueEntry struct {
    Provider    string    `json:"provider"`
    Profile     string    `json:"profile"`
    Machine     string    `json:"machine"`
    AddedAt     time.Time `json:"added_at"`
    Attempts    int       `json:"attempts"`
    LastAttempt time.Time `json:"last_attempt"`
    LastError   string    `json:"last_error"`
}

func (q *SyncQueue) Add(provider, profile string, failedMachines []string)
func (q *SyncQueue) ProcessPending(ctx context.Context) ([]*SyncResult, error)
func (q *SyncQueue) Clear()
func (q *SyncQueue) RemoveOld(maxAge time.Duration)
```

### Queue Processing
Process queue on `caam sync` or periodically during other operations:
```go
func processQueueIfNeeded(ctx context.Context) {
    state, err := LoadSyncState()
    if err != nil || !state.Pool.Enabled {
        return
    }
    
    // Clean up old entries (> 24 hours)
    state.Queue.RemoveOld(24 * time.Hour)
    
    if len(state.Queue.Entries) == 0 {
        return
    }
    
    // Process queue in background
    go func() {
        results, _ := state.Queue.ProcessPending(ctx)
        logSyncResults(results, nil)
    }()
}
```

### Rate Limiting (Throttling)
Prevent sync storms if many changes happen quickly:
```go
type SyncThrottler struct {
    lastSync    map[string]time.Time // key: "provider/profile"
    minInterval time.Duration        // Default: 30 seconds
    mu          sync.RWMutex
}

func NewThrottler(minInterval time.Duration) *SyncThrottler

func (t *SyncThrottler) ShouldSync(provider, profile string) bool {
    t.mu.RLock()
    defer t.mu.RUnlock()
    
    key := provider + "/" + profile
    if last, ok := t.lastSync[key]; ok {
        if time.Since(last) < t.minInterval {
            return false // Too soon, skip
        }
    }
    return true
}

func (t *SyncThrottler) RecordSync(provider, profile string) {
    t.mu.Lock()
    defer t.mu.Unlock()
    t.lastSync[provider+"/"+profile] = time.Now()
}
```

### Sync History Logging
```go
type SyncHistory struct {
    Entries   []SyncHistoryEntry `json:"entries"`
    MaxSize   int                `json:"max_size"` // Default: 1000
}

type SyncHistoryEntry struct {
    Timestamp time.Time         `json:"timestamp"`
    Trigger   string            `json:"trigger"` // "backup", "refresh", "manual", "retry"
    Provider  string            `json:"provider"`
    Profile   string            `json:"profile"`
    Machine   string            `json:"machine"`
    Action    string            `json:"action"`  // "push", "pull", "skip"
    Success   bool              `json:"success"`
    Error     string            `json:"error,omitempty"`
    Duration  time.Duration     `json:"duration"`
}

func (h *SyncHistory) Add(entry SyncHistoryEntry)
func (h *SyncHistory) Recent(limit int) []SyncHistoryEntry
func (h *SyncHistory) ForMachine(machineName string, limit int) []SyncHistoryEntry
func (h *SyncHistory) Errors(limit int) []SyncHistoryEntry
```

## Enabling Auto-Sync

### First Time Setup
```bash
$ caam sync enable

Auto-sync is now enabled. When you backup or refresh tokens,
they will automatically sync to machines in your sync pool.

Current sync pool: 0 machines
Add machines with: caam sync add <name> <address>

Tip: Use 'caam sync status' to check sync pool status.
```

### With Machines Already Added
```bash
$ caam sync enable

Auto-sync is now enabled.
Will sync with 3 machines: work-laptop, home-desktop, cloud-vm
```

### Disabling
```bash
$ caam sync disable

Auto-sync is now disabled.
Use 'caam sync' to manually sync when needed.
```

## Configuration
In `~/.caam/config.yaml`:
```yaml
sync:
  # Note: enabled and auto_sync are stored in sync state, not config
  # These are just defaults for new sync pools
  min_interval: 30s      # Minimum time between syncs for same profile
  retry_attempts: 3      # Max retry attempts for failed syncs
  retry_backoff: 5m      # Wait between retries
  queue_max_size: 100    # Max pending sync entries
  history_max_size: 1000 # Max history entries
```

## Status Feedback

### During Backup/Refresh (when auto-sync is enabled)
```bash
$ caam backup claude alice@gmail.com
Backed up claude auth to profile 'alice@gmail.com'
  Vault: /Users/jeff/.local/share/caam/vault/claude/alice@gmail.com
Syncing in background... (use 'caam sync status' to check)
```

### Sync Status Shows Pending
```bash
$ caam sync status
...
Background syncs: 1 in progress
Queue: 0 pending
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Auto-sync defaults to OFF (requires explicit enable)
- [ ] Sync triggers after backup command (when enabled)
- [ ] Sync triggers after refresh command (when enabled)
- [ ] Sync runs in background (non-blocking)
- [ ] Failed syncs are queued for retry
- [ ] Queue is processed on manual sync
- [ ] Old queue entries cleaned up (> 24h)
- [ ] Rate limiting prevents sync storms
- [ ] Sync history is logged
- [ ] Status feedback during backup/refresh
- [ ] `caam sync enable/disable` works correctly
- [ ] Config controls retry behavior
- [ ] Integration tests for trigger flow

## Files to Modify
- `cmd/caam/cmd/backup.go` - Add sync trigger
- `cmd/caam/cmd/refresh.go` - Add sync trigger
- `cmd/caam/cmd/activate.go` - Add sync trigger (optional)

## Files to Create
- `internal/sync/trigger.go` - Trigger logic
- `internal/sync/queue.go` - Queue management
- `internal/sync/throttle.go` - Rate limiting
- `internal/sync/history.go` - History logging

## Dependencies
- Depends on: caam-3wx (sync infrastructure)
- Depends on: caam-683 (SSH transport)
- Depends on: caam-npy (sync algorithm)
- Part of epic: caam-2bm (Multi-Machine Vault Sync)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:45:15.82848476-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T15:02:26.151573842-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-zxk","depends_on_id":"caam-3wx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:47:33.495725755-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-zxk","depends_on_id":"caam-683","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:47:38.601321838-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"caam-zxk","depends_on_id":"caam-npy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:47:43.708150233-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

```bash
$ caam backup claude alice@gmail.com
Backed up claude auth to profile 'alice@gmail.com'
  Vault: /Users/jeff/.local/share/caam/vault/claude/alice@gmail.com
Syncing in background... (use 'caam sync status' to check)
```

### Sync Status Shows Pending
```bash
$ caam sync status
...
Background syncs: 1 in progress
Queue: 0 pending
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Auto-sync defaults to OFF (requires explicit enable)
- [ ] Sync triggers after backup command (when enabled)
- [ ] Sync triggers after refresh command (when enabled)
- [ ] Sync runs in background (non-blocking)
- [ ] Failed syncs are queued for retry
- [ ] Queue is processed on manual sync
- [ ] Old queue entries cleaned up (> 24h)
- [ ] Rate limiting prevents sync storms
- [ ] Sync history is logged
- [ ] Status feedback during backup/refresh
- [ ] `caam sync enable/disable` works correctly
- [ ] Config controls retry behavior
- [ ] Integration tests for trigger flow

## Files to Modify
- `cmd/caam/cmd/backup.go` - Add sync trigger
- `cmd/caam/cmd/refresh.go` - Add sync trigger
- `cmd/caam/cmd/activate.go` - Add sync trigger (optional)

## Files to Create
- `internal/sync/trigger.go` - Trigger logic
- `internal/sync/queue.go` - Queue management
- `internal/sync/throttle.go` - Rate limiting
- `internal/sync/history.go` - History logging

## Dependencies
- Depends on: caam-3wx (sync infrastructure)
- Depends on: caam-683 (SSH transport)
- Depends on: caam-npy (sync algorithm)
